description: Encyclopedia-style article about a historical topic
content:
The History of the Internet

The Internet originated as ARPANET in the late 1960s, a project funded by the United States Department of Defense. The initial goal was to create a decentralized communication network that could survive partial outages.

Key milestones include the development of TCP/IP protocols in 1983, the creation of the World Wide Web by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989, and the release of the Mosaic browser in 1993 which made the web accessible to non-technical users.

By 2025, over 5.5 billion people worldwide had access to the Internet, fundamentally transforming commerce, communication, education, and entertainment.
